title: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room
short: A procurement trust question becomes a proof room with evidence, boundaries, and reviewer validation.
problem: Enterprise and sovereign buyers ask whether an AI workflow system can be trusted, governed, audited, and explained. A sales deck is not enough. They need a proof room.
mission: Convert buyer due diligence into a structured Evidence Docket: claims, controls, documents, security posture, public/private boundaries, review records, and a procurement-ready trust response.
why: Procurement is where interest becomes adoption. This proof card demonstrates that trust itself can be made into a workflow, improved through proof, and reused across future enterprise conversations.
plans: reuse accepted answer in future procurement
goals: turn buyer friction into reusable capability
agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates sponsor funding, builder claim, proof submission, reviewer validation, proof-card registration, credential issuance, and reputation for procurement proof work.
rsi: Accepted procurement artifacts become future context only if the proof room survives evidence review and claim-boundary checks.
evidence: Red-team or challenge report where applicable
